title: Australia Patch 4 is available
officialSummary: Australia Patch 4 was released on 9 July 2026 and was listed as available when this entry was verified.
editorialImplication: Teams running Australia should compare their current patch level and planned maintenance window against the newly available patch.
recommendedActions: Confirm the current patch level of each instance.
Review the Patch 4 problem-fix notes and security notes available to your organization.
Validate the target patch in non-production before scheduling production.

title: Flow Designer upgrade regressions are fixed
officialSummary: The July Australia-compatible release fixes Flow Designer data pills, a subflow test freeze involving dependent inputs, and removes JavaScript reference qualifiers for a security problem.
editorialImplication: The release is especially relevant to teams blocked by Australia upgrade regressions or maintaining flows that use dependent inputs.
recommendedActions: Identify affected flows and subflows in development.
Re-run automated and manual flow tests after updating.
Review custom reference qualifiers for assumptions affected by the security change.

title: UI Builder authoring defects are fixed
officialSummary: The July UI Builder release addresses several authoring defects, including repeated server-script execution, incomplete guided tours, Interaction Builder branching problems, and an inability to add the Calendar component.
editorialImplication: Teams experiencing unstable authoring or unexpected server work should prioritize a focused regression test of their most complex pages.
recommendedActions: Retest pages using server-script steps, custom dialogs, and Interaction Builder branches.
Verify Calendar component authoring where it was previously blocked.

title: Platform Analytics receives broad dashboard fixes
officialSummary: Version 8.4.1 fixes dashboard data being overwritten, date and timezone filtering errors, lost work notes during drilldown refreshes, permission errors, and several navigation and accessibility defects.
editorialImplication: This is a broad maintenance release for organizations relying on dashboards for operational decisions; date-sensitive and editable dashboards deserve regression coverage.
recommendedActions: Retest business-calendar, date-filtered, and multi-tab dashboards.
Validate drilldowns from Service Operations Workspace with unsaved work notes.
Confirm dashboard permissions with representative non-admin users.

title: Usage Insights can measure cross-application journeys
officialSummary: Usage Insights adds conversation analytics, cross-application conversion funnels, session- and user-based funnels, no-code event tracking, Platform Analytics integration, and accessibility improvements.
editorialImplication: Product teams can measure journeys that cross portals or workspaces without treating each application as an isolated experience.
recommendedActions: Choose one high-value cross-application journey for a funnel pilot.
Define the events and success outcome before enabling additional tracking.

title: ITSM changes its default AI model selection
officialSummary: ITSM skills and agents no longer default to Now LLM; each defaults to an optimized small third-party model, while large third-party models require approval.
editorialImplication: Model behavior, data-governance expectations, approvals, cost, and output quality may differ even when the user-facing skill appears unchanged.
recommendedActions: Inventory enabled ITSM skills and their configured models.
Confirm third-party model approval and governance requirements.
Benchmark output quality and latency with representative incidents before rollout.

title: Service Portal gains in-form ticket deflection
officialSummary: ITSM Foundation adds in-form ticket deflection in Service Portal, using AI to classify intent, retrieve knowledge, and suggest a resolution before submission.
editorialImplication: The capability could reduce avoidable demand, but only if suggestion quality and user escape paths are measured carefully.
recommendedActions: Pilot with a bounded request population.
Measure deflection, abandonment, reopen, and escalation rates.
Review knowledge coverage and feedback paths before wider adoption.

title: SOW related-list operations are fixed for Zurich
officialSummary: The July Zurich-compatible release fixes problems adding interactions to incidents and approvers to requested items through their related lists.
editorialImplication: Zurich customers using these related-list workflows can target a small but operationally visible defect fix without waiting for a family upgrade.
recommendedActions: Confirm the installed SOW ITSM Applications version.
Retest both related-list operations with fulfiller personas after updating.

source.title: Service Operations Workspace ITSM Applications release notes

title: CMDB MCP server moves to a separate application
officialSummary: The CMDB MCP server is removed from the Now Assist for CMDB plugin and its registry records are inactive; ServiceNow provides the CMDB MCP and Visibility Server as a separate application.
editorialImplication: Existing integrations that assume the MCP server is delivered by the Now Assist plugin need a dependency and installation review.
recommendedActions: Inventory CMDB MCP consumers and registry records.
Evaluate and approve the separate CMDB MCP and Visibility Server application.
Regression-test authentication, tools, and downstream agent workflows.

source.title: Now Assist for CMDB release notes

title: CMDB adds topology-aware impact analysis
officialSummary: Now Assist for CMDB adds topology-aware qualitative impact analysis for change and incident records associated with configuration items.
editorialImplication: This may improve triage and change assessment where CI relationships are trustworthy; weak topology data will limit its value.
recommendedActions: Pilot with services that have strong ownership and relationship quality.
Compare generated impact analysis with expert assessments.
Track incorrect or unsupported conclusions before broader use.

source.title: Now Assist for CMDB release notes

title: CMDB governance and remediation defects are fixed
officialSummary: CMDB Workspace fixes disabled editing for single-CI Data Manager tasks, inconsistent remediation access on de-duplication tasks, and missing location hierarchy information in Conditional Search.
editorialImplication: The update removes friction from common governance and remediation workflows rather than introducing new configuration.
recommendedActions: Retest single-CI review, de-duplication remediation, and location-based Conditional Search.
Confirm the fixes with the operational teams that reported the original symptoms.

title: Service Observability adds MetricBase data mappings
officialSummary: Service Observability can use MetricBase running on Agent Client Collector as a data source for data mappings; default dashboards still contain only CMDB-based data unless customized.
editorialImplication: Metric availability does not automatically produce useful visualizations, so adoption includes dashboard design and validation work.
recommendedActions: Confirm ACC and MetricBase prerequisites.
Prototype the required custom dashboard charts.
Validate metric freshness and mapping quality before operational use.

title: Strategic Planning expands portfolio controls
officialSummary: Strategic Planning adds backlog and hierarchy access for teams connected to Collaborative Work Management, dependency change views, spillover calculations, persistent hierarchy expansion, and on-demand regeneration for Portfolio AI Insights.
editorialImplication: The release can reduce planning friction, but changes to calculations and AI refresh behavior should be reconciled with portfolio operating practices.
recommendedActions: Select one portfolio to test spillover calculations and dependency views.
Document when Portfolio AI Insights should be regenerated and by whom.
Validate permissions for connected CWM teams.

title: Project Workspace redesigns RIDAC navigation
officialSummary: Project Workspace replaces the flat RIDAC grid with dedicated AI-identified risk, type-specific, and all-RIDAC views.
editorialImplication: The redesigned navigation may improve risk review but changes familiar operating procedures and training material.
recommendedActions: Walk project managers through the three new RIDAC views.
Update screenshots and instructions that reference the flat grid.
Confirm saved working practices remain possible.

title: Goal Framework adds status rollups
officialSummary: Goal Framework for SPM adds status rollup from target breakdowns to cumulative targets and from targets to goals.
editorialImplication: Automated rollups can reduce manual status work, but calculation and exception rules need to match the organization's goal-governance model.
recommendedActions: Model a representative cumulative target hierarchy in sub-production.
Reconcile rolled-up status with the existing executive reporting method.

source.title: Goal Framework for SPM release notes
